reports.title: Muted Reaction On Print, Likely Due To Capex, But Growth Overhangs Are Lifting
reports.headline: MSFT Azure/Copilot momentum improved, but higher capex/FCF pressure likely muted reaction; Buy, $540 PT maintained.
reports.summary: TD Cowen views MSFT's qtr as a growth turning point: Azure growth/guide improved, Copilot adoption stepped up, and AI ARR reached $37b, reducing overhangs on AI monetization. The offset is materially higher capex, with CY26 capex now seen at $190b vs prior $154b and FY27 FCF modeled near breakeven, tempering NT enthusiasm despite Buy rating and unchanged $540 PT.
important_observations: Azure grew 39% cc and JunQ guide of 39-40% cc beat Street at ~37%, with mgmt expecting modest accel in 2HCY26.
Copilot reached >20m paid seats, +~5m q/q, suggesting adoption is finally inflecting after a slower prior ramp.
AI ARR surpassed $37b, +123%, now ~17% of Commercial Cloud ARR, supporting the view that AI rev is becoming material.
Capex intensity is the key debate: TD now models capex rising from ~31% of rev in FY25 to ~64% in FY27E, pressuring FCF.
MSFT will continue leaning on OpenAI IP, with royalty-free access guaranteed through 2032, reducing concern that capex is for building its own frontier models.
new_data: Q3 rev $82.9b, +15% cc, vs Street $81.4b.
Q3 EPS $4.27 vs Street $4.05 and TD prior $4.04.
Azure +39% cc vs guide 37-38% and Street 38%.
M365 Commercial Cloud +15% cc; Copilot paid seats >20m vs 15m last qtr.
Total capex $31.9b vs TD est $35.9b; JunQ guide >$40b; CY26 capex now $190b.
Commercial bookings -6% cc incl Azure commitments from OpenAI, after +228% cc last qtr.
Cloud GM ~66%, down ~300 bps y/y but 1 pt above guide.
management_insights: Demand continues to outstrip Azure supply, expected to persist through CY26.
Azure upside driven by better inference efficiencies and faster capacity onboarding; Fairwater began onboarding 6 weeks ahead of schedule.
Mgmt expects Azure growth to modestly accel in 2HCY26 vs 1HCY26.
Mgmt highlighted Copilot usage intensity, with weekly engagement on par with Outlook and 1st-party agent MAU up 6x q/q.
Mgmt cited higher component pricing as ~$25b of CY26 capex increase and a headwind to PC market demand.
estimate_changes: FY26 rev raised to $329.7b from $328.3b; EPS raised to $17.36 from $17.17.
FY27 rev raised to $387.3b from $383.6b; EPS cut slightly to $19.24 from $19.30.
Q4 FY26 rev raised slightly to $87.8b, but EPS cut to $4.22 from $4.25 on weaker MPC and margin pressure.
CY26 capex est raised to $190b from $154b; FY27 FCF now near breakeven.
PT maintained at $540, based on ~25x CY27 EPS of ~$21.

reports.title: Carvana Co: 1Q26 Reaction: All Systems Go
reports.headline: 1Q26 beat on units, GPU, SG&A leverage and Adj EBITDA; MS says result restores confidence in profitable growth trajectory.
reports.summary: MS views CVNA's 1Q26 print as a broad beat that likely clears NT investor concerns around margin durability, profitable growth, and retail GPU pressure. Adj EBITDA of $672mn beat cons/MS, retail units grew 40% y/y, retail GPU beat a lowered bar, and SG&A/unit improved y/y and q/q. Mgmt reiterated FY26 significant growth and the LT target of 3mn units/yr at 13.5% Adj EBITDA margin in 2030-35, while MS keeps OW and $450 PT.
important_observations: 1Q26 Adj EBITDA of $672mn beat cons ~$646mn and MSe $640mn, supporting bull case on operating lev despite competitive and macro concerns.
Retail used units of 187,393 were +40% y/y and 4% above cons, addressing demand/growth worries.
Retail GPU of $3,165 beat cons ~$3,101; non-GAAP retail GPU of $3,250 beat cons ~$3,173 and buy-side bar near $3,100.
SG&A/unit fell to $3,682, down $314/unit y/y and $152/unit q/q, showing continued cost leverage.
Variable Adj EBITDA margin improved to 8% vs 7% in 4Q25, easing concerns after 4Q raised questions on profitable growth.
Buy-side FY26 bar appears above cons: mid/high-30% retail unit growth and low/mid-$3bn Adj EBITDA vs cons +29% and $2.90bn.

1Q26 retail used rev $4.83bn vs cons $4.40bn, +10%.
1Q26 diluted EPS $1.69 vs cons $1.42, +19%.
1Q26 net income $405mn vs cons $351mn, +15%.
Gain-on-sale spread compressed modestly to ~9.0% from 9.2% in 4Q25, better than MSe 8.6%.
Shares were up ~10% after-hours/at writing.
management_insights: Mgmt guided 2Q26 sequential increases in retail units sold and Adj EBITDA.
Mgmt reiterated FY26 significant growth in both retail units and Adj EBITDA, without giving prescriptive FY guide.
Mgmt reiterated LT operational target of 3mn units/yr at 13.5% Adj EBITDA margin from 2030-35.
LT conviction tied to expanding production locations, ADESA integrations, and accel reconditioning output.

reports.title: Q&A Our Way: Noisy Quarter but Fundamental Outlook Remains Strong
reports.headline: Noisy 1Q26 miss attributed to flu/weather; UBS says fundamentals intact, Buy/PT $635 unchanged.
reports.summary: UBS reiterates Buy on HCA with unchanged $635 PT after 1Q26 adj EBITDA missed cons by $72mn despite rev slightly above cons. The report frames the miss as largely transient, with $180mn flu/weather EBITDA headwind partly offset by $120mn GA/TX supplemental payment benefit; adjusted admissions would have been 2.3% vs reported 1.3%, within HCA's 2-3% target. Mgmt maintained FY guide, March volume/cost exit rates improved, and UBS made only minor EPS est changes while highlighting pending FL/VA DPPs as potential upside not in guide.
important_observations: 1Q EBITDA miss appears explainable by known flu/weather headwinds rather than demand or margin deterioration.
Adj admissions +1.3% reported would have been +2.3% ex 70 bps flu and 30 bps weather, within HCA's target range.
Mgmt said March volume and cost trends give confidence in FY guide and 2Q starting point.
Core pricing ex supplemental payments was ~1.6-1.7%, in line/slightly above implied FY guide midpoint.
Pending FL and VA DPPs are not in guide and UBS estimates potential annualized EBITDA upside of ~$280mn and ~$100mn, respectively.
Buybacks slowed to ~$1.6bn in 1Q26 from ~$2.5bn/qtr in 2025, but UBS sees scope to step up after stock weakness.
new_data: 1Q26 adj EBITDA $3.802bn, $72mn below cons; rev $19.109bn, $29mn above cons.
Flu/weather created estimated $180mn EBITDA headwind; GA DPP/TX ATLIS added $120mn in 1Q.
Supplemental payment FY outlook improved by $200mn, from -$250mn to -$450mn previously to -$50mn to -$250mn now.
GA/TX ATLIS 1Q benefit included nearly two-thirds out-of-period items tied to 2025.
ER visits +0.3% reported; would have been +2.2% after flu/storm adjustments.
Specialty vols: IRF +5.3%, psych -2.6%, births -1%.
management_insights: Mgmt views 1Q moving parts as consistent with original guide and expects next 3 qtrs largely in line with plan.
Mgmt remains on track for $400mn 2026 resiliency agenda savings, though 1Q savings were muted by volume shortfall and slower labor flex.
Tech/AI spend stepped up in 2025 and again in 2026; could rise modestly in 2027 before stabilizing later in decade as benefits ramp.
Clinical wage inflation remains stable around 3%; contract labor relatively flat y/y.
Professional fees remain pressured, up 11% y/y, especially hospital-based physician fees.
Tariffs are fluid but currently viewed as manageable.

reports.title: Let the Gaming Begin: GSEs Will Start Accepting Loans Scored with VantageScore 4.0
reports.headline: Wells cuts FICO PT to $1,650 from $2,300 but reiterates OW after GSEs move toward accepting VS 4.0-scored loans.
reports.summary: Wells views Fannie/Freddie’s limited rollout of VS 4.0 as a neg development for FICO, but expects most lenders to keep using FICO and likely pull both FICO and VS over time to optimize borrower rates under separate LLPA grids. The firm cuts FICO ests, mainly FY27 EPS/rev, assuming no mortgage price increase next year due to higher competitive threat, and lowers PT to $1,650 from $2,300 while maintaining OW.
important_observations: GSEs will initially accept VS 4.0 via limited rollout with 21 lenders; broader availability comes later while non-participants must keep using Classic FICO.

FICO 10-T historical score data expected this summer, with 10-T availability later; initial unavailability is a slight neg because VS scores more consumers than Classic FICO and roughly matches 10-T coverage.

FICO 10-T direct license pricing of $0.99/score plus $65 closed-loan funding fee under performance model may reduce VS 4.0 appeal since lender per-score cost is same and funding fee can be passed to consumer.
Wells expects most lenders to use both scores at pre-qual and close to find best rate, rather than dropping FICO.

FHFA/Pulte comments about targeting bureaus next raised investor concern over bi-merge/single-merge credit reports, but Wells thinks lenders still likely pull all three files given small incremental cost vs $400k+ mortgage risk.

reports.title: Fair Isaac: Giving Credit Where It’s Due; 2QF26 Beat as Mortgage Pricing Power Drives Step-Change in Fundamentals
reports.headline: FICO 2QF26 beat on mortgage Scores pricing/vol upside; JPM raises EPS ests but cuts PT to $1,225 from $1,325, stays Neutral.
reports.summary: JPM says FICO’s 2QF26 showed a step-change in fundamentals from mortgage Scores pricing power, with rev +39% y/y, Scores rev +60%, mortgage origination rev +127%, and adj EPS of $12.50 beating JPMe. Mgmt raised FY26 guide, but JPM still views guide as conservative and lifts FY26/FY27 EPS ests. Debate remains centered on durability of mortgage credit-score pricing as VantageScore 4.0 rolls out at GSEs/FHA and FICO pushes Score 10T, UltraFICO, and direct licensing/performance pricing.
important_observations: Scores pricing is now driving material operating leverage: adj OM expanded >700 bps y/y to 64.7%, above JPMe 62.6%.
Mortgage Scores rev rose +127% y/y to $300mm, 72% of B2B Scores rev, helped by FICO mortgage score price doubling to $10 from $4.95.
FICO’s direct licensing program has 3 of top 5 tri-merge resellers signed and engagement with resellers covering ~90% of US mortgage volume, pending FHFA signoff.
JPM thinks FY26 guide remains highly conservative; JPM FY26E rev/EPS sit ~5%/~10% above guide with half the FY left.
Software is improving: ARR +10% y/y to $789mm and Platform ARR +49% y/y, helped by wins, usage expansion, vols, and migrations.
Stock remains Neutral despite beat/raise due to policy/channel pushback and risk VantageScore adoption caps FICO pricing power.

reports.title: 1Q26 Recap: Ticking All the Boxes
reports.headline: CVNA 1Q beat and 2Q guide support continued 40%+ unit growth, Retail GPU rebound, and Jefferies Buy thesis despite muted YTD stock perf.
reports.summary: Jefferies reiterates Buy on CVNA after 1Q26 rev/EBITDA beat cons by 6%/4%, with retail units +40% y/y for the 6th straight qtr and Retail GPU rebounding q/q after 4Q softness. 2Q guide implies retail units up at least 31% y/y, sequential EBITDA growth, and sequential Retail GPU growth, which Jefferies sees as addressing fears of a sharper decel from 3P data. Model is under review; PT remains $475.
important_observations: 40% y/y retail unit growth for a 6th straight qtr reinforces LT share-gain thesis in the $800B used-car market.
2Q unit guide implies at least +31% y/y growth, helping counter concerns from 3P data showing a sharp exit-rate decel in 1Q.
Retail GPU rebounded 6% q/q after 4Q recon-cost issues, suggesting unit economics remain resilient.
Mgmt highlighted operational fixes at underperforming recon facilities; labor hours/unit recently near all-time lows despite limited rollout of new tools.
Top/bottom quartile recon facility Retail GPU gap stayed around $200, implying both top-quartile gains and remaining bottom-quartile improvement runway.
Ad/unit expected roughly consistent, implying ad spend grows about 40% y/y as CVNA invests in awareness/trust.

Retail units +40% y/y and 4% above cons.
Retail vehicle rev/unit 5% above cons.

EBITDA $672M; EBITDA margin 10.4%.
Gross profit 4.5% above cons, partly offset by SG&A 3% above cons.

management_insights: Mgmt attributed Retail GPU rebound mainly to improved operational intensity at bottom-quartile facilities.
New initiatives include centralized staffing planning/decision-making, AI/labor allocation tools, and stronger training/workforce development.
Mgmt noted tools have only been introduced at a couple facilities, leaving further rollout upside.
2Q Retail GPU expected down y/y due to tougher tariff-related comp, lower shipping fees, higher recon costs, and atypically low wholesale/retail spread.

The note raises EPS materially, modestly lifts rev/EBITDA, pulls forward the 3mn-unit target to 2032 from 2033, and argues CVNA's brand, traffic, survey, and structural cost data support accelerating share gains despite NT GPU pressure, higher capex, and auto-credit risks.

reports.title: Muted Reaction On Print, Likely Due To Capex, But Growth Overhangs Are Lifting
reports.headline: MSFT Buy, $540 PT; Azure/Copilot growth overhangs lifting, but capex/FCF pressure tempers NT reaction.
reports.summary: TD Cowen maintains Buy and $540 PT on MSFT after a solid MarQ beat and above-Street Azure guide, arguing the qtr marks a turning point as Copilot adoption inflects and Azure growth visibility improves. The offset is materially higher AI capex, with CY26 capex now seen at $190b vs $154b prior and FY27 FCF near breakeven, likely explaining the muted stock reaction despite stronger growth signals.
important_observations: Azure grew 39% cc, modestly above guide/Street, but demand remains supply-constrained through CY26.
JunQ Azure guide of 39-40% cc was above Street at ~37%, helped by inference efficiency gains and faster capacity onboarding.
Copilot paid seats reached >20m, up ~5m q/q, a step-up after slow adoption over the prior 2+ yrs.
MSFT now expects modest Azure accel in 2HCY26, reducing a key growth overhang.
Capex reset is material: CY26 capex now $190b, with higher component pricing contributing ~$25b.
FCF profile weakens sharply; TD now models capex rising from ~31% of rev in FY25 to ~64% in FY27E.
AI ARR reached $37b, ~17% of Commercial Cloud revs, suggesting AI monetization is becoming material.
new_data: MarQ total rev $82.9b, +15% cc, vs Street $81.4b.
MarQ EPS $4.27 vs TD prior $4.04 and Street $4.05.
Azure +39% cc vs guide 37-38% cc and Street 38% cc.
M365 Commercial Cloud +15% cc, 1 pt above guide and +1 pt q/q accel.
Copilot paid seats >20m vs 15m last qtr; 50k+ seat customers up 4x y/y.
AI ARR surpassed $37b, +123%, vs $13b disclosed in 2Q25.
MarQ capex $31.9b vs TD $35.9b; JunQ guide >$40b.
management_insights: Demand continues to outstrip Azure supply, expected to remain true through CY26.
Mgmt cited better inference efficiencies and quicker capacity onboarding as drivers of stronger Azure guide.
Fairwater started onboarding in early Apr, ~6 weeks ahead of schedule; MSFT again added 1GW in MarQ.
Mgmt expects M365 Commercial Cloud normalized growth of 15-16% cc in JunQ.
Mgmt said Copilot weekly engagement is now on par with Outlook and usage intensity is improving across agents/reasoning features.
Mgmt expects Azure modest accel in 2HCY26 vs 1HCY26.
Mgmt noted AI GM levels are trending better than early Azure 1.0 cloud buildout margins.
estimate_changes: FY26 rev raised 0.4% to $329.7b; FY26 EPS raised 1.1% to $17.36.
FY27 rev raised 1.0% to $387.3b; FY27 EPS cut 0.3% to $19.24.
JunQ rev raised 0.3% to $87.8b, but EPS cut 0.9% to $4.22.
CY26 capex fcst raised to $190b from $154b, driving weaker FCF outlook.
PT unchanged at $540, based on ~25x CY27E EPS of ~$21.

evidence_quality: Company-reported results/guidance: primary evidence from MSFT MarQ print and JunQ segment guide; high weight for reported rev, EPS, Azure, capex, and Copilot metrics.
Mgmt call commentary: primary but qualitative; useful for supply constraints, capacity timing, inference efficiency, and 2HCY26 Azure accel claims.
Analyst model work: TD Cowen estimate revisions and FCF/capex forecasts are analyst inference, sensitive to AI demand and component cost assumptions.
Market/consensus data: FactSet consensus used for beat/guide comparisons; helpful context but not primary operating evidence.
Proprietary survey work: TD references AI & Cloud survey support for thesis, but details are not included in this note, so weight is limited here.
risks_or_watch_items: Higher capex may keep pressuring FCF and investor appetite even if growth accel continues.
Azure remains supply constrained through CY26; capacity onboarding timing is critical.
Component price inflation, especially memory, is pressuring capex and PC-market outlook.
MPC guide is weak, with Windows OEM/Devices and Gaming expected to decline in JunQ.
Dynamics growth may slow as buyers adjust to new seat + consumption pricing structures.
IC GM compression from AI infra scaling remains a watch item.
key_numbers: Rating Buy; PT $540; price $424.46 as of 2026-04-29.
MarQ rev $82.9b, +15% cc, vs Street $81.4b.
MarQ EPS $4.27 vs Street $4.05.
Azure +39% cc; JunQ guide 39-40% cc vs Street ~37%.
M365 Commercial Cloud +15% cc; Copilot paid seats >20m, +5m q/q.
AI ARR $37b, +123%, ~17% of Commercial Cloud revs.
MarQ capex $31.9b; JunQ capex guide >$40b; CY26 capex fcst $190b.
FY27E FCF near breakeven; capex modeled at ~64% of rev in FY27E.
Valuation cited at ~20x CY27E P/E.

reports.title: Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN): Q1’26 Review: Investing Behind Secular Growth Themes Across Compute, Commerce & Advertising
reports.headline: GS reiterates Buy and raises AMZN PT to $325 from $275 after Q1 beat, higher AWS/backlog confidence, and raised FY26-28 ests despite elevated capex/FCF pressure.
reports.summary: GS frames AMZN’s Q1’26 as a broad beat/raise setup: rev exceeded guide/Street, AWS reaccel’d to +28% y/y with backlog evidence supporting sustained 30%+ growth, Ads remained >20%, and op income beat on efficiency and topline strength. GS raises FY26-28 rev/EPS/EBIT ests and PT to $325, arguing AMZN offers one of the best r/r profiles in coverage as eComm margin expansion, Ads scale, and GenAI-driven AWS demand offset NT capex and margin headwinds.
important_observations: Q1 rev of $181.5bn was above guide high end and +2.4% vs GS/cons, with upside across Online Stores, 3P seller services, Ads, subs, AWS, NA and intl.
AWS grew +28% y/y, >400 bps reaccel, with backlog/revenue commitments cited as support for sustained 30%+ y/y AWS rev growth in forward qtrs.
Trainium momentum is a key variant point: chips business +40% q/q, ~$20bn ARR, >$225bn Trainium commitments, and much of Trainium 4 already reserved.
Retail quick commerce is scaling rapidly, incl perishables sales up >40x y/y, same-day 1- and 3-hour delivery on 90k+ items, and Amazon Now sub-30-min delivery.
Q1 op margin hit 13.1%, described as AMZN’s highest ever, with op income +17% vs GS and +14% vs cons.
GS sees AMZN mgmt leaning into investment ahead of demand across AWS/AI, logistics, delivery speed, and satellite connectivity, arguing ROI concerns should be lower given demand signals.
new_data: Q1 total rev $181.5bn, +17% y/y, +2.4% vs GS/cons, above $173.5-178.5bn guide.
AWS rev $37.6bn, +28% y/y and +1.9% vs GS.
Ads rev $17.2bn, +24% y/y and +1.1% vs GS.
Q1 GAAP op income $23.9bn, 13.1% OM, +17% vs GS and above $16.5-21.5bn guide.
Q1 GAAP EPS $2.78 vs GS $1.55 and cons $1.64; EPS included ~$16.8bn unrealized investment gain.
Capex elevated in FY26, pressuring FCF, as mgmt invests for AI/AWS growth.
management_insights: Mgmt highlighted strong demand signals in AWS backlog, custom silicon, Trainium, and AI customer choice via offerings such as Bedrock.
Mgmt emphasized AMZN’s history of investing capital ahead of large market opps when forward demand signals are strong.
Mgmt reiterated elevated FY26 capex to capitalize on AI demand and support LT AWS rev growth.
Forward margin commentary flagged SBC seasonality, ~$1bn Leo scaling costs in 2026, and higher transportation costs from fuel inflation.
estimate_changes: Buy reiterated; 12m PT raised to $325 from $275.
FY26 rev raised to $827.6bn from $798.3bn; EPS raised to $9.02 from $7.72; EBIT raised to $109.0bn from $103.7bn.
Q2’26 rev raised to $197.6bn from $186.9bn, but EPS cut to $1.76 from $1.81 and EBIT cut to $23.8bn from $24.4bn.
FY27/FY28 rev, EBIT and EPS all raised materially, with larger outer-year AWS and 3P seller service contributions.

evidence_quality: Company-reported results/guidance: primary evidence for Q1 rev, segment rev, op income, EPS and guide comparisons; high weight.
Management call/commentary: primary but qualitative support for AWS backlog, AI demand, capex, logistics and margin headwinds; medium-high weight.
Analyst model work: GS estimate revisions and PT methodology drive valuation conclusions; high internal consistency but dependent on GS assumptions.
Market/industry data: FactSet consensus and share price inputs used for beat/valuation context; useful triangulation.
Some Trainium/backlog and quick-commerce datapoints appear sourced from company commentary/disclosure; high relevance but need tracking for conversion into rev/FCF.
risks_or_watch_items: AWS AI capex cycle may pressure FCF and returns if demand conversion lags capacity buildout.
Forward margins face SBC seasonality, ~$1bn Leo scaling costs and fuel/transportation inflation.
Consumer softness could pressure eComm vols and discretionary demand.
Ads growth sustainability and competitive share in retail media/programmatic remain key debates.
Cloud/eComm competition, regulatory changes, and investor risk appetite for growth stocks could weigh on multiple.
Q2 EBIT/EPS cuts despite rev raise suggest NT margin volatility.

FY26E FCF -$19.1bn; FY27E FCF -$45.7bn; FY28E FCF -$2.2bn.

Valuation: ~25x GS 2027 GAAP EPS at after-hours price of $270; PT uses blended EV/EBITDA, FCF-SBC DCF, and SOTP.

reports.title: Carvana Co | Master Class in Execution: Back to the Flywheel, PT to $510
reports.headline: MS reit OW and raises PT to $510 from $450 after 1Q beat, higher FY26-28 ests, stronger conviction in profitable unit growth and consumer moat.
reports.summary: Morgan Stanley views CVNA's 1Q as a clean beat that restores confidence after prior qtr margin concerns, with mgmt pointing to centralized planning, better ops tools, workforce development, and still-unrecognized efficiency gains. MS keeps OW, raises PT to $510, pulls forward the 3mn unit guide to 2032 from 2033, lifts FY27 EBITDA by LSD%, and argues CVNA's structural GPU advantage, brand/traffic momentum, and operating leverage support continued share gains despite NT GPU pressure and higher capex.
important_observations: 1Q beat plus mgmt commentary lowered concern that CVNA must sacrifice retail GPU to grow units ~40%.
2Q retail GPU bar reset lower, but MS sees 2H recovery intact and FY26 GPU largely unchanged at ~$3,280, down only 1.2% y/y.
MS expects upside to 2026 unit growth: MSe +37% y/y vs cons +29%, with potential toward 40%.
CVNA's adjusted GPU advantage vs KMX rises to >$700/unit in 2026e, giving room to compete on price while preserving margins.
AlphaWise survey shows CVNA taking share from 'CarMax only' users; CVNA-only users rose to 38% from 31% Sep-25, while KMX-only fell to 40% from 44%.
Web traffic and app download data show CVNA engagement surpassing KMX since 2H24 and widening into 2026.
Net leverage improved to ~1.1x net debt/Adj EBITDA vs 1.2x in 4Q, 1.4x in 3Q, and 2.8x in FY24.
new_data: 1Q26 EPS actual $1.69 vs prior FY26 quarterly ests showing Q2 $2.09, Q3 $2.52, Q4 $2.20.
Mar-26 web traffic hit 30.3mn for CVNA vs 22.5mn for KMX; CVNA highest in dataset.
Last month app downloads reached 218k for CVNA and 163k for KMX.
AlphaWise survey: CVNA leads KMX among users by 7ppts on pricing, 8ppts on app/web ease, and 5ppts on selection.
ABS data: CVNA subprime DQs elevated vs group, but defaults in line/below peers on lower FICO basis; 2024/2025 vintages better than 2022/2023.
management_insights: Mgmt attributes execution to centralized planning/decision-making, better day-to-day ops tools, and workforce development.
Mgmt said labor hours per unit in recent weeks are near all-time highs, implying efficiency benefits not yet fully in financials.
Mgmt expects 2Q retail GPU up q/q but down y/y due to tariff benefit non-repeat, lower shipping fees, higher non-vehicle costs, and narrower wholesale-to-retail spreads.
Mgmt expects overhead expense stable in absolute dollars and ad spend stable per unit.
Mgmt reit 6-8 new ADESA integrations in 2026; full build-outs at select sites start in 2Q.
estimate_changes: PT raised to $510 from $450; bull case to $825 from $745; bear case to $150 from $130.
FY26/27/28 total rev raised modestly to $27.9bn/$36.5bn/$47.2bn.
FY27 Adj EBITDA raised 3% to $4.45bn; FY28 raised 4% to $5.99bn.
FY26 capex raised to $245mn from $216mn, above cons ~$208mn.

evidence_quality: Company-reported results/guidance and mgmt commentary are primary evidence for 1Q beat, GPU guide, opex, capex, ADESA timing, and leverage.
Analyst model work drives est/PT changes; high transparency in line-item revisions but valuation depends heavily on LT DCF assumptions and multiple expansion.
AlphaWise proprietary survey and web/app datasets triangulate consumer moat/share-gain thesis; useful but survey/alt-data signals can be volatile.
Market/industry data from Refinitiv, FactSet, Intex, SimilarWeb, SensorTower, Google Trends, and Morning Consult support valuation comps, traffic, brand, and ABS observations.
ABS credit discussion is market-data based and mixed: DQs elevated in places, defaults/vintages more supportive, so watch credit trends closely.
risks_or_watch_items: 2Q retail GPU y/y decline from tariff benefit non-repeat, lower shipping fees, higher non-vehicle costs, and narrower spreads.
Margin sustainability if CVNA passes cost savings to customers to drive share gains.
Capex step-up as ADESA full build-outs begin; FY26 capex now $245mn vs prior $216mn and cons ~$208mn.
Worsening auto credit conditions, higher DQs, credit availability, and used-car prices.
Competition from KMX/franchise dealers and possible higher ad/logistics/fuel costs.
Valuation risk: PT embeds 27.2x FY27 EBITDA and faster path to 3mn units.

FY26 retail GPU ~$3,280, -1.2% y/y; 2Q GPU revised lower to ~$3,290.
FY26 SG&A/retail unit $3,583; FY27 $3,336, $57 below prior.
